description: Baseline: uncached compinit (183ms compaudit), spaceship (147ms), mise (38ms), static evals every start

asi.path_leak: fnm adds new multishell path each nested shell

description: compinit -C always + wipe dump on home-manager switch: saves ~127ms steady-state

asi.hypothesis: skip compaudit entirely via compinit -C; wipe .zcompdump in activation script so completions stay fresh after upgrades

description: Pre-build fzf/direnv/zoxide init as nix derivations: eliminates subprocess spawns, integrations load from nix store

asi.hypothesis: pkgs.runCommand captures binary output at build time; runtime shell just sources a file
asi.note: smaller gain than expected from timing - fzf/direnv/zoxide weren't the real bottleneck

description: Switch spaceship → starship: eliminates 155ms spaceship::precompile overhead (28x dirname subprocess), init pre-built as nix derivation

asi.hypothesis: starship is rust-based with negligible init overhead vs spaceship's 28x dirname subprocess forks

description: zsh-defer for syntax-hl+autopair, mise guard for nested shells, lazy aws completion

asi.hypothesis: defer cosmetic plugins post-prompt; skip mise initial hook-env in nested shells; lazy-load aws completer
